cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the minimum number of moves a knight needs to go from a1 to h8 on a standard chessboard. Let me start by recalling how a knight moves. A knight moves in an L-shape: two squares in one direction and then one square perpendicular, right? So from any position, a knight can move to up to eight different squares, but of course, that depends on its current position on the board. Since the knight starts at a1, which is the bottom-left corner, and needs to get to h8, the top-right corner, I need to find the shortest path. Let me think about how to model this. It's a classic shortest path problem, and I can probably use Breadth-First Search (BFS) to find the minimum number of moves. BFS is good for unweighted graphs where you need the shortest path, which is the case here since each move is considered to have the same weight. Let me visualize the chessboard.
